title: श्री नारायण ज्योतिर्मय स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
ज्योतिर्मयं परं ब्रह्म नारायणमनामयम्।
सर्वज्योतिस्वरूपं तं प्रणमामि सनातनम्॥१॥

यस्य भासा भासयन्ते सूर्यचन्द्रौ हुताशनः।
तं ज्योतिषां ज्योतिराद्यं नारायणमुपास्महे॥२॥

ज्योतिरन्तर्बहिश्चैव ज्योतिर्ज्योतिरनातुरम्।
तेजसां निधिमक्षय्यं नारायणमजं विभुम्॥३॥

ज्योतिषा सर्वलोकेषु भासते यत्प्रभावतः।
सूर्ये चन्द्रे च दीपे च नारायणकलाऽखिला॥४॥

तमसः परमो धाता ज्योतिषां ज्योतिरुत्तमम्।
सदा प्रकाशमानं तं हृदि ध्यायेत् सनातनम्॥५॥

हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सत्यस्यापिहितं मुखम्।
तत्त्वं पूषन् अपावृणु सत्यधर्माय दृष्टये॥६॥

न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं
नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः।
तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं
तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति॥७॥

ज्योतिर्मयं ध्रुवं शान्तमचलं सर्वगं विभुम्।
नारायणं परं ज्योतिः शरणं यामि सर्वदा॥८॥

ज्योतिर्मयस्तवं पुण्यं नारायणपरायणम्।
यः पठेत् प्रातरुत्थाय तमोनाशो भवेत्ततः॥

॥ इति श्री नारायण ज्योतिर्मय स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
Jyotirmayam Param Brahma Narayanam Anamayam.
Sarvajyotisvarupam Tam Pranamami Sanatanam.||1||

Yasya Bhasa Bhasayante Suryachandrau Hutashanah.
Tam Jyotisham Jyotiradyam Narayanam Upasmahe.||2||

Jyotirantarbahishchaiva Jyotirjyotir Anaturam.
Tejasam Nidhim Akshayya Narayanam Ajam Vibhum.||3||

Jyotisha Sarvalokeshu Bhasate Yatprabhaavatah.
Surye Chandre Cha Dipe Cha Narayanakalaa'khila.||4||

Tamasah Paramo Dhata Jyotisham Jyotiruttamam.
Sada Prakashamanam Tam Hridi Dhyayet Sanatanam.||5||

Hiranmayena Patrena Satyasyapihitam Mukham.
Tattvam Pushan Apavrinu Satyadharmaya Drishtaye.||6||

Na Tatra Suryo Bhati Na Chandratara-kam
Nema Vidyuto Bhanti Kuto'yam Agnih.
Tameva Bhantam Anubhati Sarvam
Tasya Bhasa Sarvamidam Vibhati.||7||

Jyotirmayam Dhruvam Shantam Achalam Sarvagam Vibhum.
Narayanam Param Jyotih Sharanam Yami Sarvada.||8||

Jyotirmayastavam Punyam Narayanaparayanam.
Yah Pathet Pratarutthaya Tamonasho Bhavet Tatah.

|| Iti Shri Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ज्योतिर्मय परम ब्रह्म, रोगरहित नारायण, सर्वज्योति स्वरूप सनातन को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ।

जिनकी आभा से सूर्य, चन्द्र और अग्नि प्रकाशित होते हैं — उन ज्योतियों के आदि ज्योति नारायण की हम उपासना करते हैं।

भीतर और बाहर ज्योति, ज्योतियों की ज्योति, अविकारी, तेजों के अक्षय निधि, अजन्मा, विभु नारायण।

जिनके प्रभाव से सर्वलोकों में ज्योति भासित होती है — सूर्य, चन्द्र, दीप सब में नारायण की कला व्याप्त है।

अन्धकार से परे, ज्योतियों का उत्तम ज्योति, सदा प्रकाशमान सनातन को हृदय में ध्यान करें।

हिरण्मय (स्वर्ण) पात्र से सत्य का मुख ढका है। हे पूषन्! उस आवरण को हटाओ, सत्यधर्म के दर्शन के लिए।

वहाँ सूर्य नहीं चमकता, न चन्द्र-तारे, न विद्युत, अग्नि की तो बात क्या। उसके प्रकाशित होने से सब प्रकाशित होते हैं।

ज्योतिर्मय, ध्रुव, शान्त, अचल, सर्वगत, विभु — ऐसे परम ज्योति नारायण की शरण सदा लेता हूँ।

जो प्रातः उठकर नारायण ज्योतिर्मय स्तव का पाठ करता है, उसके सर्व तम (अन्धकार/अज्ञान) का नाश होता है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीनारायणज्योतिर्मयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य याज्ञवल्क्य ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप्-त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीज्योतिर्मयनारायण देवता। ॐ नमो नारायणाय बीजम्। ज्योतिशक्तिः। तमोनाशः कीलकम्। ज्ञानप्राप्त्यर्थे पाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Illuminates the inner self and destroys all darkness of ignorance
Contains key Upanishadic mantras on the Supreme Light
Invokes clarity of mind and spiritual vision
Traditionally recited for removal of depression, fear, and mental darkness
Powerful for meditation on the inner divine light
Traditionally associated with the direct experience of narayana as the light of all lights
description: Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram — श्री नारायण ज्योतिर्मय स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Vishnu. This revered text originates from Upanishads (Isha, Katha, Mundaka) and Traditional Compilation. Devotees recite this prayer to bring the direct experience of Narayana as the Light of all lights and illuminate the inner self and destroy all darkness of ignorance. It is also said to remove depression, fear, and mental darkness. This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Diwali, Ekadashi, and Vaikuntha Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 5 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Upanishadic hymn to Narayana as the Supreme Light for knowledge and enlightenment.
source: Upanishads (Isha, Katha, Mundaka) and Traditional Compilation
relatedSearches: Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram lyrics in Sanskrit
Stotras for inner light and self-realisation

seoFaqs.question: What does the title Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The title combines three Sanskrit terms. Narayana is one of the principal names of Vishnu, often interpreted as the one whose abode is in waters (nara) or the resting place of all beings (nara-ayana). Jyotirmaya means consisting of light, made entirely of brilliance, or radiant. Stotram is a hymn of praise. Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram is therefore a hymn that praises Narayana specifically in his aspect as the cosmic light, the self-luminous principle that pervades and underlies all visible illumination, from the sun and stars to the inner light of consciousness. The hymn invites the devotee to meditate on Vishnu not as an anthropomorphic deity but as the prior radiance from which form itself emerges.

seoFaqs.question: How does the stotram fit within the Vaikhanasa tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vaikhanasa tradition is one of the two oldest Vaishnava agama traditions of South India, the other being Pancharatra. Vaikhanasa is rooted in Vedic ritual and emphasises temple-based worship of Vishnu through carefully prescribed daily, weekly and seasonal services. Many Tirumala-Tirupati and Andhra-Tamil Nadu Vishnu temples follow Vaikhanasa agama. Hymns that meditate on Narayana as light, including Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram, fit naturally within this tradition because Vaikhanasa daily worship begins before dawn with meditation on the rising of consciousness as light, and the Vedic Narayana Suktam, which forms the core of Vaikhanasa nyasa, itself describes Narayana as the inner ruler shining within the heart.

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of meditating on Narayana as light rather than as form?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava theology holds two complementary modes of meditation: rupa-dhyana, in which the devotee visualises Vishnu's specific form (blue body, four arms, conch and discus, Lakshmi at his side), and arupa-dhyana, in which the devotee meditates on Vishnu as formless light, consciousness or being. Form-based meditation is more accessible and emotionally engaging; light-based meditation is subtler and more universalising. Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram offers the second mode, training the devotee to recognise Narayana not only in the temple icon but also in the sun overhead, in the lamp before the altar, and in the steady awareness within his own heart. Mature Vaishnava sadhana usually integrates both modes.

seoFaqs.question: How does this stotram relate to the famous Narayana Suktam of the Vedas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Narayana Suktam, found in the Mahanarayana Upanishad of the Krishna Yajur Veda, is the foundational Vedic hymn to Narayana and is recited daily in Vaikhanasa and Smarta traditions. It describes Narayana as antaryami, the inner ruler shining within the heart-cave, and invokes him as the supreme light beyond the sun. Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram draws directly on this Vedic theology of Narayana-as-light and elaborates it in the slightly more emotional, devotional register of stotra literature. Where the Suktam is austere and Vedic, the Stotram is warmer and more meditative, but the underlying vision of Narayana as the self-luminous source of all light is the same.

seoFaqs.question: When is Narayana Jyotirmaya Stotram traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: It is recited at sandhya, that is at sunrise, midday and sunset, the three transitions when Vedic and Vaikhanasa traditions hold that the meditation on inner light is most natural. It is also chanted on Ekadashi, on Karthika Pournami when the lighting of lamps and ritual snana are central, on Vaikuntha Ekadashi, and during the Brahmotsavam and other temple festivals at Vaikhanasa-tradition shrines. Householders sometimes adopt the stotram as a bedtime prayer, on the principle that one should fall asleep with the light of Narayana steady in the mind so that even sleep becomes a kind of communion. The stotram is short and quickly memorised, which makes it suitable for any of these moments.

seoFaqs.question: What inner experience does the stotram aim to cultivate?
seoFaqs.answer: Vaishnava commentators describe a three-stage inward movement. First, the devotee notices outer light: the sun, the lamp, the brightness of the world. Second, with the stotram's help, he or she traces this outer light back to its source, recognising that no created flame is the original light but only a reflection. Third, the meditation rests in the steady inner light of awareness itself, which is identified with Narayana antaryami, the indwelling Lord of the Narayana Suktam. This progressive deepening of attention from outer to inner light is the practical fruit of regular recitation. The stotram functions less as a request prayer and more as a guided meditation rendered into hymnic form.
